# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: View Public Forecast of 311 Demand by Category

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-17 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/017-uc-17-public-forecast-view/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the resident-facing needs defined beyond merely loading the page, including what must be understandable in successful views, incomplete-coverage states, and explicit failure states? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §Implementation Steps]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how the portal chooses the current approved public-safe forecast source and keeps that choice separate from generation or approval workflows? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-002a, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §Reused Source Entity: ApprovedPublicForecastVersion]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required fields of the public-safe payload fully specified, including service category, demand value or demand-level summary, forecast window label, published timestamp, and coverage messaging? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007a, Spec §FR-013, Data Model §PublicForecastVisualizationPayload, Contract §components.schemas.PublicForecastView]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for all API interactions involved in the experience, including anonymous retrieval of the current view and reporting of final client display outcomes? [Completeness, Plan §Implementation Steps, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what performance expectations apply to both successful and non-success outcomes, including unavailable data, blocked sanitization, and render failures? [Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals, Data Model §Canonical UC-17 Vocabulary]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “understandable to a public resident” translated into observable content and presentation expectations rather than left as a purely subjective outcome? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK007 Is “current approved public-safe forecast source” unambiguous about whether daily, weekly, or other upstream forecast products may qualify for the public portal at a given time? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-002a, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK008 Is “public-safety filtering rules” precise enough to distinguish when data passes as-is, must be summarized, must be blocked, or should fail the request? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-012, Data Model §Sanitization Status]
- [x] CHK009 Is the phrase “render charts, summaries, indicators, or equivalent public-facing visual elements” clear enough to define what minimum information must still appear when charting choices vary by implementation? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK010 Is “one internally consistent approved public-safe forecast version” defined clearly enough to guide request handling when the upstream approved version changes mid-request? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Implementation Steps, Data Model §Derived Invariants]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are incomplete-category-coverage requirements consistent between the clarification, edge cases, functional requirements, data model, and public contract fields for `coverage_status` and `coverage_message`? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013, Data Model §Coverage Status, Contract §components.schemas.PublicForecastView]
- [x] CHK012 Are the success-display logging rules consistent between the spec’s retrieval and display wording and the plan’s separation of portal request, payload preparation, and final display event reporting? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-005, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §PublicForecastPortalRequest, Data Model §PublicForecastDisplayEvent]
- [x] CHK013 Are anonymous-access assumptions consistent with the API contract and plan so that no hidden authentication, authorization, or session dependency is introduced on the public read path? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK014 Are the rules for sanitization and allowed output fields consistent between the spec, data model, and OpenAPI schema so restricted details cannot leak through alternative property names or nullable fields? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005 through §FR-007a, Data Model §PublicForecastVisualizationPayload, Data Model §PublicForecastView, Contract §components.schemas]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify public UX value, or do they mostly measure backend retrieval, filtering, and logging behavior without defining what residents must be able to interpret? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-005]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance targets measurable for the public load path and display-event reporting path, rather than only describing general interactive behavior? [Measurability, Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals, Contract §GET /api/v1/public/forecast-categories/current, Contract §POST /api/v1/public/forecast-categories/{publicForecastRequestId}/display-events]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement for traceable missing-data, render-failure, and successful-display records be objectively verified without a more explicit definition of required correlation fields and terminal-event semantics? [Measurability, Spec §FR-008 through §FR-010, Spec §SC-003 through §SC-005, Data Model §PublicForecastPortalRequest, Data Model §PublicForecastDisplayEvent]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for successful viewing, sanitized viewing, incomplete-coverage viewing, unavailable data, and client-side render failure? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §Implementation Steps]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for cases where the upstream approved public-safe version exists but contains no displayable category summaries after sanitization? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011, Data Model §PublicForecastVisualizationPayload, Data Model §PublicForecastSanitizationOutcome]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery expectations defined for what happens after a render failure is reported, or is the lifecycle intentionally limited to traceability without retry or replay behavior in UC-17? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Implementation Steps, Contract §POST /api/v1/public/forecast-categories/{publicForecastRequestId}/display-events]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error scenarios covered by written requirements for missing request identifiers, malformed display-event payloads, duplicate display reports, and unavailable upstream forecast versions? [Coverage, Gap, Plan §Implementation Steps, Contract §paths]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for category summaries where neither a numeric demand value nor a qualitative demand-level summary can be shown safely? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-007a, Data Model §PublicForecastVisualizationPayload, Contract §components.schemas.PublicForecastCategorySummary]
- [x] CHK023 Are concurrency requirements defined for repeated refreshes or parallel anonymous requests while the approved public-safe forecast version changes upstream? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for sanitization outcomes that detect restricted detail but remove nothing useful for display, potentially leaving the portal with no valid category content? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011, Data Model §PublicForecastSanitizationOutcome]
- [x] CHK025 Are API and UX boundaries defined for large category counts, result ordering, and payload size so the public portal remains understandable and responsive as category breadth grows? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Performance Goals, Data Model §PublicForecastVisualizationPayload]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how incomplete coverage, unavailable data, blocked sanitization, and render failures should be explained to a general public audience without exposing internal details? [Non-Functional, UX, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for portal requests, sanitization outcomes, prepared payloads, and display events, especially given anonymous access and observability needs? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §Overview, Data Model §Relationships]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for anonymous retrieval, backend sanitization, payload preparation, and display-event ingestion, not just described qualitatively as fast enough for public page load? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific enough about disclosure control, anonymous abuse boundaries, correlation-header handling, and the rule that only backend services may enforce public-safety filtering? [Non-Functional, Security, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §FR-005 through §FR-007, Plan §Constitution Check, Contract §parameters]
- [x] CHK030 Are availability and operational-safety requirements defined for how the portal behaves when upstream forecast lineage is healthy but the visualization preparation or display-event reporting path is degraded? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Gap, Spec §FR-009 through §FR-011, Plan §Implementation Steps]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on approved forecast lineage, publication workflows, and public-safe release rules explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than left as implementation assumptions? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constitution Check, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that filtering rules “exist or will be defined by the implementation” strong enough, or should the spec state the minimum public-release contract UC-17 depends on? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK033 Are external dependency requirements defined for how frontend rendering libraries, shared visualization modules, or upstream forecast stores fail, timeout, or return stale data during public requests? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Implementation Steps, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the spec’s requirement to sanitize restricted details for display and the data model rule that `sanitization_status = blocked` or `failed` is invalid when `status = available`, especially if sanitization removes all displayable content? [Conflict, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011, Data Model §PublicForecastSanitizationOutcome, Data Model §PublicForecastView]
- [x] CHK035 Is “successful public display” established clearly enough as a client-visible terminal state, or does it currently depend on the plan’s extra distinction between backend payload preparation and frontend-reported rendering success? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-005, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §PublicForecastDisplayEvent]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “approved public-safe forecast version,” “public forecast visualization payload,” “sanitization outcome,” “portal status,” and “display outcome” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and contract to support one canonical public API vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary, Data Model §Canonical UC-17 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
